tags.note: Mapped location appears accurate - As of Nov 2025, the latest Bing imagery was taken during a period of flooding (see the parking lot approx 10 deg NE of the slipway)

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points downstream
tags.name: Stump Island Boat Ramp
tags.note:
Amenities: privy restrooms right above the boat ramp
full restroom and showers past the field and pavilion 
water spigot by the pavilion, campground

tags.description: Ramp Approach: points downstream; plenty of muddy bank landing
tags.name: LaBenite Park Boat Ramp
tags.note: Access Amenities: Pit toilets, plenty parking, pavilion

tags.description: Ramp Approach: hides behind dike structures; points perpendicular to the river; muddy bank landing available
tags.name: Cooley Lake CA
tags.note: Amenities: Pit toilets

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points downstream; Rocky bank available for landing; No lights, easy to miss at night.
tags.name: Dalton Bottoms Access Boat Ramp
tags.note: Pit Latrine, camping

tags.description: 2 boat ramps pointing downstream; Plenty rocky and muddy bank landing
tags.name: Lexington Riverfront Park
tags.note: Amenities: Pit toilets

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points downstream; Swift water off of rocky bank, some mud bank in eddy past the ramp
tags.name: Miami Access Boat Ramp
tags.note: Amenities: Pit Toilets, camping

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points downstream. There is a shallow dike just upstream of ramp. Many people land on sandbar to the right (downstream) of ramp. Ramp is just upstream of bridge.
tags.name: Carl R. Noren Boat Ramp
tags.note: Amenities: Pit toilets near parking

tags.name: Hwy W
tags.note: You have to climb down big rip rap.  Not real easy but I have done it multiple times.

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points slightly downstream. Access sits just after Middle River mouth
tags.name: Mokane Access Boat Ramp
Bush Landing
tags.note: Status unknown, but there is a ramp here

tags.description: Ramp Approach: points downstream; difficult to see from river until you are right there.
tags.name: Hartsburg Access Boat Ramp

tags.description: Ramp approach: perpendicular, next to dike
tags.name: Market Street
tags.note: By KATY Trail trailhead

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points perpendicular to the river.  Access sits just after Moniteau Creek mouth
tags.name: Marion Conservation Area

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Dike structure aimed slightly downstream just before boat ramp! Must pass then turn upstream into eddy to paddle to ramp.  Dock just downstream of ramp; don't get swept into. Ramp points downstream
tags.name: Coopers Landing
tags.note: Amenities: camping available at a cost; Store with drinks and snacks

tags.description: Ramp Approach: ramp points slightly downstream. Tucked back into the bank. Difficult to see at night
tags.name: Huntsdale Access Boat Ramp
tags.note: No facilities

tags.description: Ramp Approach: points downstream, some rocky and muddy bank landing
tags.name: Fort Osage
tags.note: Amenities: no restrooms. Fort Osage County Park just up hill with restrooms.

tags.description: Ramp Approach: pointed slightly downstream, pretty swift water
tags.name: Fraklin Island CA

tags.description: Ramp Approach: ramp pointed downstream. Ramp is on right less than a mile below I-70 bridge.
tags.name: Taylor's Landing Boat Ramp

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Narrow Boat Ramp points downstream.
tags.name: USACE Napoleon Boat Ramp
tags.note: Ramp generally closed to public but open for MR340 Race. Facility owned by U.S. Army Corps of Engineers.

tags.name: Unknown Access
tags.note: This was the access for a resort that closed.  Not sure if it can  be used

tags.description: Ramp Approach: points downstream and is just downstream of the bridge.
tags.name: Waverly Access
tags.note: Restroom

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Ramp points downstream and rocky bank landing.
tags.name: Port of Waverly Park
tags.note: restroom

tags.description: 2 boat ramps pointing downstream; Plenty rocky and muddy bank landing
tags.name: Lexington Riverfront Park
tags.note: Amenities: Pit toilets

tags.description: Ramp Approach: Points slightly downstream, closer to perpendicular with the river. Trailing (parallel) dike upstream stretches almost in front of the ramp, so turn in at last minute after dike.
tags.name: Chamois Access
tags.note: Amenities:  Bathrooms and showers at pavilion a short walk from ramp. Small campground with 4 spots, must be reserved through the City of Chamois
Downgraded road since the Canton Ferry closed in 2014. See Source Reference.
